Beet Armyworm.description: A destructive caterpillar that targets a wide range of crops, known for skeletonizing leaves and boring into flowers.
Beet Armyworm.cause: Larvae chew leaves, buds, and fruits; skeletonize foliage and bore into flowers.
Beet Armyworm.solution: Use Bacillus thuringiensis, spinosad, and remove weeds to disrupt lifecycle.

Black Hairy.description: A hairy caterpillar that aggressively defoliates plants, leaving only the veins behind.
Black Hairy.cause: Defoliates plants by feeding on leaves, leaving only veins.
Black Hairy.solution: Hand-pick caterpillars, apply neem oil or insecticidal soap.

Cutworm.description: A nocturnal pest that cuts young seedlings at the base, causing sudden wilting and plant death.
Cutworm.cause: Cuts seedlings at soil level, causing wilting and plant death.
Cutworm.solution: Use soil collars, till soil, apply diatomaceous earth or light traps.

Jute Aphid.description: A sap-sucking insect that infests jute crops, leading to stunted growth and bushy tops.
Jute Aphid.cause: Sucks sap from jute leaves and stems, causing stunted growth and bushy tops.
Jute Aphid.solution: Spray dimethoate or encourage natural predators like ladybugs.

Jute Hairy.description: A caterpillar that feeds on green tissues of jute leaves, causing skeletonization and reduced photosynthesis.
Jute Hairy.cause: Feeds on chlorophyllous tissues, causing leaf skeletonization.
Jute Hairy.solution: Use light traps, microbial sprays like Bacillus thuringiensis, or Diazinon.

Jute Red Mite.description: A tiny pest that pierces leaf cells and sucks sap, leading to yellowing and poor plant health.
Jute Red Mite.cause: Pierces leaf cells and sucks sap, leading to yellowing and reduced photosynthesis.
Jute Red Mite.solution: Apply sulfur sprays, neem oil, or introduce predatory mites.

Jute Semilooper.description: A looping caterpillar that feeds on apical leaves and buds, reducing jute fiber yield.
Jute Semilooper.cause: Feeds on apical leaves and buds, reducing plant growth and fiber yield.
Jute Semilooper.solution: Brush plants with kerosinized rope or spray Sevin pesticide.

Jute Stem Girdler.description: A beetle that girdles jute stems for egg-laying, causing breakage and fiber loss.
Jute Stem Girdler.cause: Girdles stem at two levels for egg-laying, causing stem breakage and fiber loss.
Jute Stem Girdler.solution: Apply phorate to soil and conserve parasitoids like Neocatolaccus nupserhae.

Jute Stem Weevil.description: A stem-boring pest that lays eggs inside jute stems, leading to knot formation and shoot withering.
Jute Stem Weevil.cause: Bores into stem and lays eggs, causing knots and shoot withering.
Jute Stem Weevil.solution: Spray carbaryl or destroy stubble after harvest.

Leaf Beetle.description: A common pest that chews on leaves and stems, weakening plants and reducing photosynthesis.
Leaf Beetle.cause: Chews leaves and stems, reducing photosynthesis and plant vigor.
Leaf Beetle.solution: Use crop rotation, trap crops, neem oil, or biological predators.

Mealybug.description: A soft-bodied insect that secretes honeydew, promoting mold growth and stunting plant development.
Mealybug.cause: Sucks sap and secretes honeydew, leading to sooty mold and stunted growth.
Mealybug.solution: Apply neem oil, introduce ladybugs, or use alcohol swabs for small infestations.

Pod Borer.description: A larval pest that bores into pods and consumes seeds, causing significant crop yield loss.
Pod Borer.cause: Bores into pods and feeds on seeds, causing yield loss.
Pod Borer.solution: Use neem-based sprays, Bacillus thuringiensis, and hand-pick larvae early.

Scopular Emissaria.description: A moth species whose larvae feed on leaf tissues, creating holes and reducing plant productivity.
Scopular Emissaria.cause: Feeds on leaf tissues, creating holes and reducing photosynthesis.
Scopular Emissaria.solution: Apply biological control and pheromone traps to reduce population.

Termite.description: A soil-dwelling pest that damages plant roots and stems, leading to collapse and death.
Termite.cause: Damages roots and stems, causing wilting and plant collapse.
Termite.solution: Deep plowing, orange oil, neem seed bait, or entomopathogenic fungi.

Termite odontotermes.description: A species of termite that attacks underground plant parts, causing drooping and uprooting.
Termite odontotermes.cause: Attacks roots and stems, causing drooping and uprooting of plants.
Termite odontotermes.solution: Apply chlorpyrifos to soil and irrigate after broadcasting treatment.

Yellow Mite.description: A microscopic pest that distorts leaf growth by sucking sap, leading to yellowing and curling.
Yellow Mite.cause: Sucks sap from leaves, causing curling, yellowing, and stunted growth.
Yellow Mite.solution: Use neem extract, pepper spray, and introduce predatory bugs.
